description: Simulated micro green bonds tied to real Cerritos infrastructure projects. Students learn municipal finance concepts: coupon, maturity, risk, and project impact.
disclaimer: This is an educational simulation only. No real securities are being issued, purchased, or traded. No real money is involved.

projects.name: Recycled Water System Expansion
projects.description: Expand the 25-mile recycled water distribution network to serve additional parks and medians, building on the system that saves 722M gallons/year.

projects.name: Municipal Solar Phase III
projects.description: Install additional rooftop solar on city buildings, following the success of the Corporate Yard system (350,000 kWh/yr) and the historic 1978 City Hall installation.
riskFactors: Proven technology in Southern California climate

projects.name: Urban Canopy Acceleration
projects.description: Accelerate tree planting from 250 to 500 trees/year and establish a young tree maintenance program, building on Cerritos' 30,000+ tree urban forest.

projects.name: SB 1383 Organics Compliance
projects.description: Fund enhanced organics collection infrastructure and community education to meet California SB 1383 diversion targets, through the Athens Services franchise.

projects.name: COW Transit Modernization
projects.description: Upgrade Cerritos on Wheels (COW) fleet to hybrid/electric vehicles and expand service to cover 200+ locations with 45-minute headways.
riskFactors: Ridership growth depends on frequency improvements
Funded by Prop A/C — Metro funding risk

ratingScale.description: Medium quality — low to moderate risk

learningObjectives: Understand what a municipal bond is and how it funds public projects
Learn the relationship between risk, return (coupon rate), and bond rating
Calculate simple payback periods and return on investment for infrastructure
Compare trade-offs between different sustainability investments
Understand how Cerritos departments manage capital project budgets

sources.name: City of Cerritos — FY 2025-26 Adopted Budget
City of Cerritos — Current Green Efforts
